Farn Poltrtcrl Pna.crtcus C ovtnrtssloN
P.O. Bo.x u07 ' -t2ll J Strcet 'Sacrarnento. CA 95ttll-01t07
(916) ill-5660 ' Ftx (916) -122-0tt86
April26,2000
Craig S. Miller
56 Glen Road
Novato, Califomia 94945
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No.I-00-014
The Act's conflict of interest rules do not prohibit a public official from holding any
particular employment or running any particular business' However' a conflict of interest may
arise in a particular governmental decision ieiore the Aviation Commission if it is reasonably
foreseeable that thelecision will have a material financial effect on your economic interests'
Dear Mr. Miller:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")'r
QUESTTON
Must you resign either your position as a commissioner or your consulting role under the
conflict of interest rules of the Act?
CONCLUSION
FACTS
I Government Code sections g1000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear attitle 2, sections 18109 -
18996, of the Califomia Code of Regulations'
You are a commissioner for the Aviation commission for Marin county' an advisory
entitytotheboardofsuperviso.r.You.po'itionisappointedbythecountyboardofsupervisors
Generally, the advice and recommendations that are provided by 
-the Aviation
Commission relate to a broad spectrum of issues pertaining to the overall operation and


File No. I-00-014
Page2
management of the county-owned Gnoss Airfield. The Aviation Commission meets monthly,
and discussion items pertain to airport tenancy, improvements, fueling procedures, business
practices, etc. The Aviation Commission has the responsibility to ensure that all aspects of the
"irn.ta 
operation comply with federal air regulations, state laws, and county ordinances,
including business op.ruting standards and HAZMAT, fire, and safety policies' The Aviation
Commission also serves * ttt" initial oversight body for the airport master plan'
you have served on the Aviation Commission for eleven years andhave been
reappointed to your seatby four separate county supervisors. You also serve as president ofa
neighboring community association to Gnoss Airfield, which gives you an added perspective in
yo,i role aJ commissioner. you have been a shong voice for the expansion and build-out of
Gnoss Airfield and view the airfield as an integral part of the Marin County hansportation
infrastructure. During the past decade, the county has invested over $4 million into enhancing
the airfield, includinithr p'*rt *r of neighboring land for future airfield expansion. You have
supported and have recommended all expansion and enhancement efforts'
your career and avocation is in the field of management consulting. You generally work
with the public sector in the consulting services you offer. Recently, you were asked by Sunset,
Aviatiorr(,Sunset") to assist them with their strategic planning and business development'
Sunset is an air charter company and has been a major tenant of Gnoss Airfield since 1991' You
stated in a phone conversation with Commission staffcounsel that Sunset is not listed in the New
york Stock Exchange, American Stock Exchange, National Association of Securities Dealers
National Market Lii, or the pacific Stock Exchange and does not meet the financial standards
for listing on the most recently published Fortune MagaztneDirectory of the 1,000 largest U'S'
corporatins, on the New York Stock Exchange, or on the National Association of Securities
Dealers National Market List. Since approxiriately October 1999 to the present, you have been
engaged in a financial relationship with Sunset. When you first accepted the project with Sunset,
you notifred the county staff and the board of supervisors of your consulting role and financial
ielationship with this air charter company. Because Sunset is a growing company, there will be
an occasional issue that will come before the Aviation Commission that will involve Sunset in
some way. you advised the county that it is your intention to excuse yourself from voting and to
distance yourself from any matters directly or indirectly involving Sunset.
Nevertheless, a deputy county counsel has indicated to you that you should resign from
either your position as a commissioner or your consulting role with Sunset. You believe that he
is taking an extremely conservative position on this matter. It is your desire to continue to serve
,, u rrr"*ber of the Aviation Commissioner for the county. Therefore, you seek advice on this
matter although you are not inquiring about a particular governmental decision at this time.


File No. I-00-014
Page 3
Ai\ALYS$
The primary purpose for the conflict of interest provisions of the Act is to ensure that
public officials, whether elected or appointed, perform their duties in an impartial manner, free
-from 
bias caused by their own financial interests. (Section S1001O).) In furtherance of this
goal, Section g7100 of the Act prohibits apublic official from making, participating in making,
6r otherwise using his or her official position to influence a govemmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest.
Determining whether a conflict of interest exists under Section 87100 of the Act requires
analysis of the following questions2:
Are you a "public official" within the meaning of the rules?
As a member of the Aviation Commission for Marin County, you are a "member, officer,
employee, or consultant of a state or local government agency" and, therefore, are subject to the
conllict of interest provisions of the Act. (Section 82048; Regulation 18701(a).)
Are you making, participating in making, or inlluencing a governmental decision?
A public official "makes a governmental decision" when the official, acting within the
authority of nir or her office or position, votes on a matter, obligates or commits his or her
agency to any course of action, or enters into any contractual agreement on behalf of his or her
,g.".y. (Regulation 18702.1.) A public official "participates in a governmental decision" when,
actingwithin the authority of his or her position and without significant substantive or
intervening review, the official negotiates, advises or makes recommendations to the
decisionmaker regarding the governmental decision. @egulatiot18702.2.) Apublic official is
attempting to use his or her official position to influence a decision if, for the purpose of
influencing, the official contacts or appears before any member, officer, employee, or consultant
of his or her agency. (Regulation 18702.3.)
you have not identified a particular governmental decision in which you would be
participating. Under the Act, the determination that a conflict exists must be made in the context
of a specifi. gor.--ental decision and must be based on the particular facts of that decision.
Because the Act's conflict of interest analysis requires the identification of a specific decision,
we are unable to conduct a complete analysis to provide you with formal assistance. However, in
absence of your making, participating in making, or influencing a govemmental decision, simply
2 Th"se questions are based on the Act's conllict of interest analysis provided at Regulation 18700(b).
The Commission document, "Can I Vote? Conflicts of Interest Overyiew" explains the steps of this analysis and is
enclosed for your information.


File No. I-00-014
Page 4
serving on the Aviation Commission while also maintaining a financial relationship with Sunset
will not create a conflict under the Act. Nevertheless, a conflict of interest may arise depending
on the actual govemmental decision before the Aviation Commission.
What are your economic interests - 
the possible sources of a financial conflict of interest?
Under Section 87103 of the Act, there are six different types of economic interests that
may potentially pose a conflict of interest for a public official:
l) A public official has an economic interest in a business entity where the public
official either has a direct or indirect investment of $1,000 or more in the business
entity. (Section 87 1 03(a); Regulation I 8703. I (a).)
2) A public official has an economic interest in a business entity where the public
official is in any of several business relationships specified in Regulations
13703.10) through (e). (Section 87103(d); Regulation 18703.1.)
3) A public official has an economic interest in any real property in which the
public official has a direct or indirect interest worth $1,000 or more in fair market
value. (Section 87 I 03(b); Regulation 187 03.2.)
4) A public official has an economic interest in any person from whom he/she has
received income aggregating $250 within 12 months prior to the time when the
relevant govemmental decision is made. (Section 87103(c); Regulation 18703.3.)
5) A public official has an economic interest in any donor of, or any intermediary
or agent for a donor ol a gift or gifts aggregating $300 or more in value provided
to, received by, or promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the
time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(e); Regulation 18703.4.)
6) A public official has an economic interest under the "personal financial effect
rule" provided by Section 18703.5. In particular, a govemmental decision has a
personal financial effect on a public official if the decision will result in the
personal expenses, income, assets, or liabilities of the official or his or her
immediate family increasing or decreasing. (Section 87103; Regulation 18703.5.)
You have inquired and provided facts regarding only your management consultant
business. Therefore, we analyze only your economic interests derived from this business. If you
subsequently wish to seek formal assistance from the Commission regarding an identified
governmental decision, you must, at that time, provide information on any other relevant
economic interest you possess in order for us to conduct a complete analysis of whether you have
a conflict in that particular decision.


File No. I-00-014
Page 5
However, based on the information you have submitted to us, you have an economic
interest in your management consulting business in three ways. First, you have an economic
interest in your business since you hold a position of management. (Section 87103(d);
Regulation 18703.1(b).) Second, if you have a direct or indirect investment of $1,000 or more in
your business, you will have an economic interest in that business entity. (Section 87103(a);
Regulation 18703.1(a).) Lastly, assuming you have received $250 or more from your business
within 12 months prior to the time when a relevant govenrmental decision is made, you have an
economic interest in your business because it is a source of income to you. (Section 87103(c);
Regulation 18703.3.)
Similarly, if you have received $250 or more from Sunset within 12 months prior to the
time when a relevant governmental decision is made, you will have an economic interest in
Sunset for purposes of that decision. (Section 87103(c); Regulation 18703.3.)
Is your economic interest directly involved in the governmental decision?
A business entity such as your consulting business or a source of income such as Sunset
is directly involved in a decision before an official's agency when that entity or source, either
directly or by an agent:
"(l) tritiates the proceeding in which the decision will be made
by filing an application, claim, appeal, or similar request or;
(2) Is a named party in, or is the subject ol the proceeding
concerning the decision before the official or the official's agency
A person is the subject of a proceeding if a decision involves the
issuance, renewal, approval, denial or revocation of any license,
permit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the subject
person." (Regulation 1 870a. I(a).)
Since you have not identified a govemmental decision, we are unable to determine
whether the economic interests in your consulting business and in Sunset would be directly
involved under this Step Four analysis.
Nevertheless, if a business entity or source of income does not meet the above criteria,
then the entity or source is not directly involved in the govemmental decision. (Regulation
18704.1(b).)
Materiality standard - what kinds of linancial impact on your economic interests from the
decision are considered material?
If your consulting business is not directly involved in a governmental decision, you must
apply the materiality standards for an indirectly involved business entity. (Ibid.)


File No. I-00-014
Page 6
For a..small"3 business entity which is indirectly involved in a governmental decision,
the effect of the decision is material when:
,,(A) The decision will result in an increase or decrease in the
gross revenues for a fiscal year of $10,000 or more; or
@) The decision will result in the business entity incurring or
avoiding additional expenses or reducing or eliminating existing
expenses for a fiscal year in the amount of $2,500 or more; or
(C) The decision will result in an increase or decrease in the
value of assets or liabilities of $10,000 or more"'
(Regulation I 8705.1O)(7XA) - OX7)(C)')
Therefore, if a decision by the Aviation Commission for Marin County will cause a
financial effect on your management consulting business which rises to the monetary thresholds
provided at 1g705.1(bx7xA) - (bx7xc), the decision will have a "material financial effect" on
your economic interest.
Additionally, the monetary thresholds of 18705.1(bX7XA) - (bX7XC) are to be similarly
applied to a source of income which is a small business not directly involved in a decision.
(ilegulation 1g705.30)(1).) You have indicated that Sunset, a source of income to you, is such a
"o*p*y. 
Consequently, if a decision by the Aviation Commission results in one of the effects
given inRegulations 18705.1(bX7XA) - OX7)(C), then Sunset also will experience a material
financial effect from the decision.
Is it reasonably foreseeable that a governmental decision by the Aviation Commission of
Marin County will result in the materiality standard for each economic interest being met?
We are unable to analryzethis question in the absence of information on a specific
governmental decision. However, please be aware, that as each particular decision is presented
io the Aviation Commission, you should analyzewhether it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision could have a material financial effect on your management consulting business, Sunset,
or any other economic interests you may have.
3 A',"mall" business is one not listed in the New York Stock Exchange, American Stock Exchange,
National Association of Securities Dealers National Market List, or the Pacific Stock Exchange, or which does not
meet the financial standards for listing on the most recently published Fortune Magazrne Directory of the 1,000
largest U.S. corporations, on the New York Stock Exchange, or on the National Association of Securities Dealers
National Market List. These criteria are identified in Regulation 18705.1(bxl)-(bx6).


File No. I-00-014
PageT
If you have a conflict of interest, does the "public generally" exception apply? Or is the
conflict disqualifying?
If, in the future, you were to conclude that it is reasonably foreseeable that a decision by
the Aviation Commission will have a material financial effect on your management consulting
business, on Sunset, or on another economic interest of yours, you also may want to analyze
whether the "public generally" exception will apply.
This exception provides that if the reasonably foreseeable material financial effect of a
decision on the public official's economic interest is indistinguishable "from its effect on the
public generally," then the public official does not have a disqualiffing conflict of interest.
(Section 87103; Regulations 1S700(b)(7),18707(a).) This exception exists because a public
official is less likely to be biased by a financial impact on his or her economic interests when a
significant part of the community is substantially likely to feel essentially the same impact from
the governmental decision. (Dickens Advice Letter, No. A-99-228.)
In particular, the reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on a public official's
economic interest is indistinguishable from the effect on the public generally if it is also
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will affect a "significant segment" of the public "in
substantially the same manner" as it affects the public official's economic interest. @egulation
I 8707(bX1) and (b)(2).)
For decisions that affect a business entity in which the official has an economic interest,
a "significant segment" is comprised of 50 percent of all businesses in the jurisdiction or the
district the official represents, so long as the segment is composed of persons other than a single
industry, trade, or profession. @egulation 1 8707(bxl XB).)
Additionally, for decisions that affect any of the official's economic interests, a
"significant segment" will be affected if the decision will affect 5,000 individuals who are
residents of the jurisdiction. (Regulation 1 8707(b)( 1 XC).)
There are also special purpose versions of the public generally exception which may
apply in special factual situations. (Regulations 18707.1 - 18707.6.) However, none of these
appear to apply to your situation. Also, in rare cases, "exceptional circumstances" may allow the
recognition of a significant segment, within the meaning of the public generally exception, even
if the numerical thresholds otherwise established are not met. (Regulation 18707(bXlXD).)
Whether a significant segment will be affected according to these regulations depends on
the specific decision and its facts. If you come to conclude that a decision of the Aviation
Commission will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect one or more of your
economic interests, then, based on your knowledge of the decision and your jurisdiction, you
must decide whether the public generally exception applies.


File No. I-00-014
Page 8
Even if you have a disqualifying conflict of interesf is your participation legally required?
The legally required participation rule provided by Regulation 18708 applies only in rare
cases where several public officials in the same agency are simultaneously disqualified. There is
nothing to indicate that this exception is applicable to your case based on the facts you have
provided.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660. We highly encourage you to write again once you have identified a prospective
decision for which you wish to receive formal assistance.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel
C
By:
B
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

substantially the same manner" as it affects the public official's economic interest. @egulation
I 8707(bX1) and (b)(2).)
For decisions that affect a business entity in which the official has an economic interest,
a "significant segment" is comprised of 50 percent of all businesses in the jurisdiction or the
district the official represents, so long as the segment is composed of persons other than a single
industry, trade, or profession. @egulation 1 8707(bxl XB).)
Additionally, for decisions that affect any of the official's economic interests, a
"significant segment" will be affected if the decision will affect 5,000 individuals who are
residents of the jurisdiction. (Regulation 1 8707(b)( 1 XC).)
There are also special purpose versions of the public generally exception which may
apply in special factual situations. (Regulations 18707.1 - 18707.6.) However, none of these
appear to apply to your situation. Also, in rare cases, "exceptional circumstances" may allow the
recognition of a significant segment, within the meaning of the public generally exception, even
if the numerical thresholds otherwise established are not met. (Regulation 18707(bXlXD).)
Whether a significant segment will be affected according to these regulations depends on
the specific decision and its facts. If you come to conclude that a decision of the Aviation
Commission will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect one or more of your
economic interests, then, based on your knowledge of the decision and your jurisdiction, you
must decide whether the public generally exception applies.
Even if you have a disqualifying conflict of interesf is your participation legally required?
The legally required participation rule provided by Regulation 18708 applies only in rare
cases where several public officials in the same agency are simultaneously disqualified. There is
nothing to indicate that this exception is applicable to your case based on the facts you have
provided.

conflict of interest rules of the Act?
